title: zanthoxylum taediosum subsp. shaferi
intro: Zanthoxylum taediosum subsp. shaferi, also known as Shafer's Prickly Ash, is a subspecies of the Zanthoxylum taediosum plant, native to the Caribbean, specifically the island of Hispaniola. It is a member of the Rutaceae family, commonly known as the citrus family, and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain. The plant is valued for its health benefits, including its antioxidant, antimicrobial, and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate digestive issues, fever, and other ailments. Herbal preparations of Shafer's Prickly Ash can be found in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are often used to support the immune system and promote overall well-being.
